summary: The diff implements a token storage utility (tokenStorage.ts) and a centralized API client (apiClient.ts) that wraps canister calls with authorization checks. It stores the principal ID in localStorage after login via AuthGuard syncing, clears it on logout/expiry, and all listed hooks use protectedCall for mutation operations. However, the implementation stores a principal ID rather than a Bearer token, so the REQ-8 AC about attaching 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' HTTP headers is not literally fulfilled. REQ-8 also lists useWebsiteSettings as a required hook but it is not visible in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: All POST, PUT, and DELETE calls made from hooks including useWebsiteSettings use the interceptor/protectedCall.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff does not include a modified useWebsiteSettings.ts hook. While other hooks are visibly updated, useWebsiteSettings is absent from the modified files list.

missing_requirements.requirement: Each outgoing protected request includes the 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header.
missing_requirements.reason: The implementation validates principal ID from localStorage before making canister calls but attaches no HTTP 'Authorization: Bearer' header. The literal AC of attaching an Authorization header is not implemented.

hallucinated_features.description: AuthGuard component with delegation expiry check and navigation to /login
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not explicitly request creating an AuthGuard component. This is a new component added beyond the stated requirements of token storage and header attachment.

hallucinated_features.description: Session expiry metadata stored in localStorage with 30-day default TTL using auth_session_active and auth_session_expiry keys
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only requires storing the token and clearing it on logout/expiry. Storing a session active flag and expiry timestamp with a 30-day default TTL is additional logic not specified in the requirements.
